title: Builtin volumes should be owned by the UID/GID of the container
body:
When creating a building VOLUME for a container we need to create it
with the ownership/permsissions of the directory that we will be mounting on.

Signed-off-by: Daniel J Walsh <dwalsh@redhat.com>

comments.body:
Does not fix #1027 for me - now the owner of the directory is the container user, so 26:26 in my case. But it is expected to have 26:0 since that is what the directory's ownership is supposed to be when no VOLUME command is used.

Additionally the volume still has incorrect permissions of 0755 instead of 0775

@pkubatrh You were right, I think I have fixed it now.  The volume should have the same ownership/permissions of the volume it will be mounting over.
@rhatdan Can confirm this fixes my use case now. Thanks!
Homu does not seem to be working.  Merging manually.
